Cerebras did what the industry calls impossible  turned an entire       mm  wafer into one chip  Defects on silicon that big are inevitable  so they built in redundancy and custom per batch masks that route around every bad core  landing near      usable wafers  The results  chip  wafer  wafer chip  cerebras did  core landing  batch masks  redundancy custom

Here s the trap  When the chip is the whole wafer  you can t shrink your way to more SRAM and you can t make the wafer bigger  The only lever left is reallocating area  and more SRAM means less compute  A zero sum tradeoff on silicon that s already maxed out        chip  wafer  wafer bigger  tradeoff silicon  sram means  trap chip

Cerebras has an escape  wafer on wafer bonding a second wafer to add SRAM or compute  They re seriously exploring it  already showing a DRAM wafer hybrid bonded onto the WSE for more fast memory  There are real thermo mechanical and bond wave challenges to clear  but going beyond dram  wafer  memory  wafer bonding  sram compute  cerebras escape  wse  wave challenges

Floating point math is not associative  And many of the highest performance kernels split the workload among SMs and accumulate partial results in a nondeterministic order  Many AI labs just accept this  or pay a huge performance penalty for determinism  DeepSeek decided to do ai  determinism deepseek  order ai  floating point  performance kernels  workload sms

DeepSeek V  s answer  end to end bitwise deterministic and batch invariant kernels  They replaced all uses of cuBLAS with their own custom implementations in DeepGEMM  Instead of using atomics to accumulate partial results in split reduction workloads  they write partials to implementations deepgemm  uses cublas  reduction workloads  bitwise deterministic  atomics accumulate

So why doesn t replacing cuBLAS cost them performance  Because DeepSeek s custom kernel stack  DeepGEMM   TileLang  is actually faster  Pre compiled cuBLAS leaves real wins on the table  build your own with many more tile sizes available for better SM occupancy  JIT compilation ai  cublas cost  performance deepseek  tile sizes  kernel stack

Jensen showing Rubin Ultra as an MCM was the real tell  This is not just Nvidia gluing more dies together because it feels like the next cool architecture move  It is what happens when reticle limits  power density  yield  and package economics all start forcing the same answer  nvidia  yield  nvidia gluing  ultra mcm  showing rubin  package economics  happens reticle

MCM is the natural answer because the alternative is worse  CoW with multiple die stitching and scaling runs into area scalability limits and yield pain  But MCM is not free either  Now you are trading one problem for another  massive substrate complexity  more advanced assembly  ai  yield  massive substrate  cow multiple  mcm free  die stitching  scaling runs

That is the deeper signal from Rubin Ultra  AI scaling is no longer just a transistor story or even a wafer story  It is becoming a packaging logistics story  The frontier is shifting from  how much compute can Nvidia design   to  how much compute can the packaging stack wafer  nvidia  packaging  ai  ai scaling  nvidia design  transistor story  compute packaging  rubin ultra

Nvidia published DWDP  Distributed Weight Data Parallelism   a new inference parallelism strategy focused on prefill  It sounds slightly insane until you remember the target machine is GB    NVL    The core trade  spend more peer GPU bandwidth so you spend less time waiting at nvidia  gpu  ai  inference  data parallelism  peer gpu  nvidia published  target machine  focused prefill

On the InferenceX dot com benchmark   K input  K output  DeepSeek R  on NVL     output TPS GPU improves      average at comparable TPS user in the        range  However  TTFT gets worse because fewer context GPUs lower the aggregate context side service rate   When you re already gpu  inference  benchmark  k  gpus lower  ttft gets  nvl   output  inferencex

DWDP is a specific optimization  you need NVL   class peer bandwidth  context heavy workloads  enough compute window to hide prefetch  and enough imbalance that sync is already hurting you  MoE inference is becoming a market for coordination tricks  NVL   make the  spend inference  dwdp specific  peer bandwidth  prefetch imbalance  nvl   make  moe inference

Instead of sending tokens to experts  each rank in DWDP prefetches the missing expert weights from peers and runs the MoE layer locally   This resembles FSDP which shards weights across GPUs  all gathers them before each layer  computes  then discards   However  FSDP uses gpu  dwdp prefetches  fsdp shards  moe layer  gpus gathers  expert weights

Whether DWDP wins depends if the compute window long enough to hide the weight prefetch  If compute finishes first  you get a bubble  If compute takes longer  the prefetch is fully hidden  Just like FSDP   At batch size    the crossover happens around   K input tokens  and bigger dwdp wins  prefetch compute  fsdp batch  bubble compute  size crossover
